title: Pseudopilocereus machrisii

It belongs to the genus Pseudopilocereus, which is a group of cacti closely related to the genus Pilosocereus. The classification of Pseudopilocereus machrisii is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its stem shape, areole arrangement, and flower structure. Within the Cactaceae family, Pseudopilocereus machrisii is part of the tribe Pilosocereae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between Pseudopilocereus machrisii and other related species within the cactus family.

intro: Pseudopilocereus machrisii, also known as Machris' pitaya cactus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae, native to Venezuela. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Pseudopilocereus machrisii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
